description: Confounder: The model assumes any t plugged into min_snr_5_weight behaves like the SNR=5 cas

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at timestep t = 1/(1+√5).' does NOT hold here. The model assumes any t plugged into min_snr_5_weight behaves like the SNR=5 case if it looks like a valid timestep, but the function cosine_sample_median returns t = 1 - cos(π/4) ≈ 0.2929, which yields SNR ≈ ((1−t)/t)² ≈ 14.07 — far above 5 — so min(snr, 5.0) caps it at 5, yet the denominator uses the *true* SNR (not the capped value), giving weight = 5 / (14.07 + 1) ≈ 0.332, not 5/6.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at timestep t = 1/(1+√5).' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
